# ANALYSIS: MIDDAY_TRENDLINE_GATE_Grinder_A_vs_B_Sweep_Result

**Filed:** 2026-05-31
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)
**Type:** sweep_result

## Summary

The queued Grinder A vs B sweep for MIDDAY_TRENDLINE_GATE compared two selectivity-gate implementation variants (A and B) on the 307-trade OOS dataset. Variant B (the gated configuration) achieved +7.2/trade P&L versus Variant A (ungated) at +3.8/trade, representing an +89% lift in per-trade expectancy. Total OOS P&L rose from +1,166/c to +1,562/c (highest among all tested configurations). Win rate improved from approximately 48% to 56% (exact WR requires per-trade logs; estimate based on P&L lift and average win/loss sizes). The gated variant correctly suppressed the 4/29 12:15 loser and preserved the 5/04 +53.6/c winner, while reducing midday loser conversions from 24/32 to an estimated 8/32.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline.
2. **Sample bias:** OOS dataset = 307 trades across 345 days; selection based on time-window filter; overfit risk mitigated by walk-forward and real-fills validation.
3. **Out-of-sample:** OOS PASS (see leaderboard entry).
4. **Real-fills:** Real-fills OOS COMPLETE (307 trades).
5. **Failure modes:** Worst day: potential over-suppression of valid entries in low-volatility regimes; max drawdown scenario: extended chop period causing missed entries; blow-up scenario: none (filter only removes trades).
6. **Concentration:** top-5 days = 51% of P&L (as reported in leaderboard).

## Pre-merge gate

Grinder A vs B sweep result must be reviewed; gym validators must pass; J Rule 9 weekend ratification.

## Confidence

8 / 10 -- based on completed OOS real-fills and clear per-trade lift.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

Complements rank 17 (V14E_BEAR_TIME_OF_DAY_GATE) and does not conflict with any existing candidates; improves upon the baseline MIDDAY_TRENDLINE_GATE filter concept.
